Anyone noticed that the Hong Kong Police recently upgraded their "flashlights" to this Acebeam W30 below?

Judging from the shape of the torch and the illumination size and intensity, it's highly probable the HK police are using this patented LEP torch named Acebeam W30.


Video clip:
https://youtu.be/3lgolXi7nhY?t=420

So I dig deeper, according to the official product site of W30, it is already not named as a flashlight but a "white laser emitter"
http://www.acebeam.com/w30

The light beam was so intense that it was classified as a Class 2M laser, and warning "DO NOT aim at person. DO NOT aim at aircrafts or vehicles in operations." was clearly listed on the website.

Even Hong Kong Government's Electrical and Mechanical Services Department listed out "Never aim a laser beam at a person's eyes" in their safety guidelines to use a Class 2M laser product.

https://www.emsd.gov.hk/en/other_regulatory_services/laser_safety/publications/safety_guidelines_for_laser_products/

So basically, while condemning the use of laser pointers against police officers (they named laser pointers as laser guns), Hong Kong Police themselves point high power laser beams at Hong Kong citizens' eyes and journalists' cameras everyday.
